claim: An N-formyl group on M3 molecules suppresss strong binding to the second position of the groove.

result.claim: An N-formyl group on M3 molecules suppresss strong binding to the second position of the groove.
result.reasoning: The initial search for similar propositions and subsequent search for similar chunks did not yield any direct evidence supporting or refuting the claim that "An N-formyl group on M3 molecules suppresses strong binding to the second position of the groove." The search results contained propositions and chunks related to binding, grooves, and suppression in general, but none specifically mentioned N-formyl groups, M3 molecules, or their combined effect on binding at a specific groove position. Therefore, there is insufficient evidence in the knowledge base to verify this claim.

evidence.text: The additional new CDR-phospholipid binding site was at an alternative groove comprised of and CDR-H2, CDR-H3, and FR-L2 we termed the Loading Site.
